{"idea": {"Name": "geometric_quantization_routing", "Title": "Geometric Quantization Routing: PAC-Bayes Stability Bounds for Zero-Training Mixed-Precision Inference", "Short Hypothesis": Quantization-induced degradation in LLMs is not uniform but concentrated in activation subspaces with high curvature and low stability radii. By monitoring online covariance condition numbers and Fisher discriminant ratios, we can predict quantization sensitivity without training, enabling a zero-overhead router that dynamically assigns precision per layer/token. This direction is necessary because trained routers (e.g., RL-based) introduce opaque overhead and violate strict latency budgets, while uniform quantization ignores structural sensitivity. The best setting is latency-critical, single-GPU inference where wall-clock constraints forbid iterative optimization or policy training.
